dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: src/lib/utils/characterData.ts imports from '../../stores' (client Svelte stores), violating boundary: utilities should not depend on framework-specific reactive state
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: 40+ files with zero importers marked as orphaned across src/lib/db/, src/lib/utils/, src/routes/api/
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: Sibling load() functions use inconsistent type signatures: ServerLoad vs PageServerLoad with varying parameter patterns
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: Empty catch blocks in src/auth.ts catch {} without any logging or recovery
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: Minimal pass-through wrappers earn their indirection (cn delegates to clsx+twMerge)
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: sharp (^0.34.4) in production dependencies but zero imports
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: Token refresh logic in bnetAccount.ts has clear branching with early returns and precise conditions
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: API routes manually destructure and validate request.json with repeated boilerplate validation - no centralized validation
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: 11 test files across 580+ source files - test-to-source ratio ~1:50
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: Error response pattern inconsistency: POST endpoints use raw Response(JSON.stringify({error}), {status, headers}) while GET endpoints use json({error}, {status})
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: Auth pattern is consistent: all protected endpoints check session?.user?.id via locals.getSession()
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Identical try-catch-console.error boilerplate across 20+ functions in src/lib/db/ (users.ts, recents.ts, userRecents.ts)
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: Two coexisting database schemas: src/lib/db/schema.ts (402 LOC, marked orphaned but actively imported) and src/lib/db/userSchema.ts (new, 6 importers)
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: src/components/ contains 22+ Svelte files at root level mixing layout (seo, footer, header), keystone-calc (apiForm, keystoneTable, scoreExplanation), log-analysis (logBrowser, logBrowserResult, yourR
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: Module-level mutable state in src/lib/utils/tokenCache.ts: let cachedToken: AccessToken | null = null shared across all requests, called by 17 API routes
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: Foundational architecture is clean: layered structure (routes → lib → db), no cycles, auth properly centralized
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: Two exports named addUserRecent with incompatible signatures: recents.ts (userId, item: RecentItem) vs userRecents.ts (userId, type, entityId, entityData, title, subtitle, metadata)
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: scoreFormula switch on stars has cases 1, 2, 3 with no default — implicit fallback to timeAdjustment=0
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: Unvalidated JSON parsing in 11+ API routes (cast-events, death-events, damage-events, boss-events, healing-events, fights, player-details, recent-reports, recent-characters, browse-logs, current-state

review_issue_summaries.summary: Utility function (characterData.ts) imports from client-side Svelte stores, violating framework-agnostic abstraction boundary
40+ files with zero importers; ownership and purpose undefined
src/lib/utils/ mixes 17 files across 5+ unrelated domains
Data schema defined in 3 locations, unclear which is current
API endpoint naming uses 3 different patterns inconsistently
Two separate database connection functions with unclear distinction
Sibling routes use inconsistent load() signatures: ServerLoad vs PageServerLoad
Sibling API routes handle GraphQL errors inconsistently
Database initialization functions use inconsistent naming despite identical implementation
Empty catch block in auth.ts completely suppresses background roster sync errors
API handlers discard original error details, returning generic 500
Similar operations use contradictory strategies: throw vs null vs empty array
GraphQL errors detected in some handlers but not others
Error handlers fall back to secondary sources without signaling degradation
Error logging is inconsistently applied
Error responses returned to clients have inconsistent shapes and status codes
Generic 'do' prefix in doSave callback hides intent
Generic callback name 'hide' provides no semantic information
Abbreviation 'res' inconsistent with codebase convention 'response'
Generic 'process' in processSeriesData masks transformation intent
Generic 'process' in processData obscures filtering and aggregation
Generic 'temp' prefix in tempScore unclear
Identical token request/expiry logic in oauth.ts and blizzardOauth.ts
4 event handlers share identical request validation, token fetch, error handling - only query template differs
GraphQL query construction repeated across handlers; no shared template
Unused production dependency 'sharp' (^0.34.4) creates bloat with zero code references
Unused production dependency '@internationalized/date' with zero references
Double setLoading(false) in nested finally blocks in recentCharacters.ts
Double setLoading(false) in nested finally in recentReports.ts
Overlapping loading state mutations when add() calls await loadFromAPI()
Dead variable tierSuffix defined but never referenced
Input validation repeated across all API routes with no centralized middleware
Session fetched via HTTP fetch in hooks instead of direct function call
Token caching via module-level singleton not safe for serverless
Fresh postgres client per request - workaround for initialization coupling
Some handlers translate external API responses, others pass raw
All 20+ API route handlers lack test coverage
OAuth token request, caching, refresh logic completely untested
Data fetching utilities and transformations lack coverage
Component tests verify DOM presence but not behavioral correctness
API responses never tested with real component rendering
Error handling branches in API routes never tested
All database CRUD operations and queries lack test coverage
Svelte stores managing app state untested
Test pyramid inverted: shallow unit tests only, no integration/e2e
Error responses use two distinct patterns: json({error},{status}) and raw new Response(JSON.stringify({error}),{status,headers})
Similar operations accept parameters in different orders: character-score (region,realm,name) vs blizzard/raiderio (name,region,realm)
Similar endpoints return data with inconsistent wrapping: direct arrays, {runs:[]}, {seriesData}, {deathEvents}
API routes lack JSDoc documenting failure modes; character-score has 6+ retry chains with undocumented error behavior
addUserRecent and similar db functions accept entityData: any and metadata: any at critical boundaries

currentState.saveToAPI wraps its fetch in a `const doSave = async () => {…}; doSave();` IIFE for no reason
scoreFormula rebuilds an affix-breakpoint lookup on every call, and computeRunLevelsForScore has an unreachable trailing return

dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Strong, consistent conventions: camelCase functions, descriptive domain nouns (getRaidStats, extractWarcraftLogsReportCode, raiderIoRealmCandidates, keystoneCalculations, hideWowheadTooltips), and cle
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: src/lib/auth/oauthBase.ts and src/lib/auth/tokenCacheBase.ts are earned shared abstractions: requestClientCredentialsToken() + createTokenCache() factory each have two real callers (WCL and Battle.net
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: requireSession (src/lib/server/requireSession.ts) + apiError/apiOk (src/lib/server/apiResponses.ts) define a single, narrow auth seam: every protected /api/* handler I sampled (recent-characters, rece
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Only 11 test files exist for ~104 production source files; the entire src/routes/api/* tree (22 +server.ts handlers) has zero tests despite handling auth, payments-adjacent score lookups, and external

review_issue_summaries.summary: README.md is empty — top-level project contract is missing
src/lib/db/connection.ts factory is dead code; getRaidDb and getUserDb still duplicate it inline
src/lib/db/index.ts barrel-exports user-DB modules, conflating the documented two-database boundary
routes/api uses three naming patterns (resource-events, verb-resource, provider-passthrough) with no documented rule
src/components/ and src/lib/components/ui/ are two component roots with no documented dividing line
src/lib/utils.ts and src/lib/utils/ coexist as same-named utility surfaces
src/lib/db/bnetAccount.ts and src/lib/db/userCharacters.ts have zero importers — domain code with undefined ownership
src/lib/hooks/ and src/lib/actions/ are empty/near-empty, implying scaffolding that never landed
character-reports top-level catch converts thrown errors into apiOk({reports:[], guilds:[]}), hiding failures from clients and contradicting the dbOperation contract
API handlers split between handleApiError() and a manually paired logServerError + apiError; both produce the same shape but exist as parallel patterns
Several non-DB modules swallow errors with empty/anonymous catches and no logging, making failures invisible
Server-side modules outside src/lib/server/ and src/lib/db/ bypass logServerError and emit free-form console.error strings, breaking the documented log shape
WCL upstream failures return 502 from handleWclFightRequest but 500 from sibling handlers that inline their own try/catch
api/blizzard returns the raw error.message to the client, while sibling handlers return generic strings — inconsistent and an info-leak risk
doSave callback uses generic 'do' prefix that adds no semantic information
`res` abbreviation diverges from project convention `response` in deathHotspots fetch
Local `hide` arrow inside hideWowheadTooltips obscures that it runs both API and DOM clears
death-events/+server.ts duplicates handleWclFightRequest plumbing instead of calling the shared factory.
createDrizzlePostgres factory in src/lib/db/connection.ts is unused; both DB wrappers inline the full client config
hooks.server.ts reads the session by self-fetching /auth/session over HTTP instead of calling Auth.js directly
browse-logs casts request.json() directly to its typed shape while sibling routes validate before trusting the body
Core rating math (keystoneCalculations.ts) has no tests despite being the product of the rating-calculator route
All 22 SvelteKit API +server.ts handlers are untested — no request validation, error path, or upstream-failure coverage
OAuth and token-cache modules (Blizzard + base) have no tests
performance.test.ts measures in-file fabricated workloads against wall-clock thresholds; tests nothing real and is timing-fragile
drizzleConfigs.test.ts re-states the literal config strings instead of validating behavior
formIndex.test.ts only verifies a barrel module re-exports its own symbols
Page and ContentShowcase tests assert exact marketing copy and Tailwind class fragments — high churn, low signal
dbIndex.test.ts only asserts a function exists, providing no real coverage of the data layer
No cross-module / load-function tests exist — server load functions and client store interactions are unverified

dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: structure.coupling_matrix has only two cross-directory edges in the entire src tree: src/lib/auth/ -> src/lib/types/ (1) and src/lib/db/ -> src/lib/server/ (1); the second edge comes solely from src/l
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: README.md is empty (0 bytes) — the top-level project contract a new engineer would read first does not exist; PRODUCT.md/DESIGN.md exist but no entry doc explains what this repo is or how it boots.
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: Naming is uniformly camelCase across src/, lib/auth/, lib/calculations/, lib/data/, lib/db/, lib/server/, lib/stores/, lib/ui/ (244 names sampled, 0 snake_case functions). Verb prefixes are consistent
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: src/lib/server/logger.ts defines logServerError/logServerWarn/handleApiError with a documented '[scope] message' shape and a JSDoc that explicitly tells route handlers to use handleApiError at every t
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Verb-prefix conventions are consistent across the lib: fetch* for HTTP/data retrieval (fetchReportsForCharacter, fetchRuns, fetchWowSummary, fetchRegion, fetchWithTimeout), get* for synchronous/db loo
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: src/lib/server/wclGraphQL.ts defines handleWclFightRequest as a real factory: 4 sibling routes (boss-events, cast-events, damage-events, healing-events) all delegate body parsing, executeWclQuery, 400
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json runtime deps are small and purposeful: 12 entries (@auth/core, @auth/drizzle-adapter, @auth/sveltekit, chart.js, chartjs-plugin-annotation, chartjs-plugin-zoom, clsx, drizzle-orm, postgre

dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Shared seam library is well-designed: apiError/apiOk in src/lib/server/apiResponses.ts unify the response shape; logServerError/handleApiError in src/lib/server/logger.ts unify the error log shape; re
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Only 11 test files exist for the entire codebase (src/lib/__tests__/*.ts and src/components/__tests__/*.ts).
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: Strong shared foundations: every src/routes/api/*/+server.ts uses apiOk/apiError from $lib/server/apiResponses, giving a single error envelope `{error: string}` and a uniform success path; auth-protec
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: Single shared helper src/lib/server/requireSession.ts is used by every user-scoped API route: character-reports, current-state (GET+POST), my-wow-characters, recent-characters (GET+POST), recent-repor
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: src/lib/server/wclGraphQL.ts exposes handleWclFightRequest, which is consumed by every per-fight WCL endpoint (boss-events, cast-events, damage-events, healing-events, death-events, etc.) — the route

dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: src/lib/ contains both a file utils.ts (cn helper, shadcn type aliases) and a folder utils/ (clientLog.ts) — two siblings sharing the same name with unrelated contents force a developer to disambiguat

dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: src/lib/db/index.ts (getRaidDb) and src/lib/db/userDb.ts (getUserDb) duplicate a ~30-line postgres() config block (prepare:false, max:1, idle_timeout:5, connect_timeout:10, transform:undefined, types:
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: src/lib/db/_helpers.ts documents the dbOperation contract explicitly (success returns typed value; failure logs once and rethrows; empty results only on successful zero-row queries) and every db/*.ts
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: src/routes/api/character-reports/+server.ts: GUILD_RECENT_REPORTS_QUERY and GUILD_REPORTS_QUERY are byte-identical GraphQL strings; fetchReportsForGuild loops over [first, second] but the second itera
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: tsconfig.json sets strict: true, and most production modules rely on inferred Drizzle types or hand-written interfaces in src/lib/types/* rather than `any` (only ~5 production hits for explicit `any`)

review_issue_summaries.summary: src/lib/db/index.ts barrel re-exports user-DB modules, eroding the documented two-database boundary at the import surface
Documented single-factory architecture in src/lib/db/connection.ts is not enforced — getRaidDb and getUserDb each inline the full client config
src/stores.ts at the top-level src/ duplicates the role of $lib/stores/, forcing components to cross the lib boundary via ../../stores imports
README.md is empty — repo has no top-level contract for a new engineer.
src/lib/db/index.ts barrel-exports user-DB modules, conflating the documented two-database split.
src/lib/db/connection.ts factory is unused; getRaidDb and getUserDb still inline the same options.
src/components/ and src/lib/components/ui/ are two component roots with no documented dividing rule.
src/lib/utils.ts and src/lib/utils/ coexist as same-named utility surfaces.
src/routes/api/ uses three naming idioms (event-type, provider passthrough, aggregation/verb) with no documented rule.
src/lib/hooks/ is empty and src/lib/actions/ has a single file — implied conventions that never landed.
src/lib/db/bnetAccount.ts and src/lib/db/userCharacters.ts are feature data-access modules with one importer each, lodged in shared infra.
death-events POST hand-rolls the WCL request lifecycle while every sibling WCL endpoint delegates to handleWclFightRequest.
DB-backed /api endpoints emit snake_case response fields while WCL-backed /api endpoints emit camelCase, with no documented contract.
character-reports outer catch returns apiOk({reports:[],guilds:[]}), making thrown errors indistinguishable from a real empty result
Half of the API handlers inline `logServerError(...) + apiError(...)` instead of the documented handleApiError helper, creating two parallel idioms with identical output
WCL upstream failures return HTTP 502 via handleWclFightRequest but 500 from sibling handlers that inline their own try/catch around the same WclQueryError
api/blizzard returns the raw error.message to the client, leaking upstream Blizzard payload bodies and diverging from sibling handlers' generic messages
Server-side modules outside src/lib/server/ and src/lib/db/ use bare console.error and empty catches, bypassing logServerError and breaking the documented log shape
Client-side store backends use raw console.error strings instead of the existing logClientError helper, leaving the client log shape inconsistent with the documented server one
doSave inner closure in saveToAPI uses the generic 'do' prefix and adds no semantic information beyond its enclosing function name
deathHotspots.svelte uses `res` for a fetch response while every other fetch site in the codebase uses `response`
Local `hide` closure in hideWowheadTooltips bundles API + DOM clears and runs three scheduled passes; the bare name obscures the multi-step retry intent
death-events/+server.ts duplicates handleWclFightRequest plumbing instead of using the shared factory the four sibling event routes adopt.
oauth.ts and blizzardOauth.ts each re-export isTokenExpired with no consumers — pass-through indirection that adds ceremony without value.
src/routes/api/top-players/+server.ts duplicates a 10-column select block, keeps unused diagnostic state, has a dead empty branch, and uses `any` in a per-request dedup loop
fetchReportsForGuild loops over two GraphQL query strings that are byte-identical and short-circuits on the first catch, so the apparent 'try second query on failure' fallback never runs
src/routes/api/browse-logs/+server.ts uses `let` for arrays/builders that are never reassigned, mixing real reassignment with declaration noise
handleSession in src/hooks.server.ts wraps a network call without a try/catch and rebuilds the CSP array every request
Every DB-backed +server.ts owns its own row→snake_case API mapper; no shared boundary translator
raiderio/+server.ts is the only external-fetch route without the standard try/catch + logServerError seam
Catch-block error translation alternates between handleApiError() and logServerError()+apiError() with no rule
src/hooks.server.ts (CSP + Auth.js handle) has no tests despite running on every request
No test harness exists for SvelteKit +server.ts handlers (no msw, no RequestEvent helper), making the 22-handler coverage gap self-reinforcing
performance.test.ts asserts `process.memoryUsage().heapUsed` growth < 50MB and depends on optional `global.gc`
API response field naming is split between snake_case (DB endpoints) and camelCase (others) with no documented rule
/api/character-score returns the success body shape with HTTP 400, breaking the codebase-wide `{error:string}` error contract
POST endpoints inconsistently validate JSON bodies: WCL endpoints type-guard rigorously while user-state mutators destructure unchecked
Sibling external-proxy endpoints handle failures inconsistently: blizzard wraps with try/catch, raiderio is unguarded
/api/blizzard returns raw upstream error text in the error envelope while sibling proxies return generic messages
`fightFilter='kills_no_deaths'` means different things in /api/spec-top-players vs /api/top-players
Authenticated POST handlers skip request-body validation that public POST siblings apply, splitting the trust boundary
Failure branch dumps five named response payloads with console.error({ ... }) instead of using the project logger.
SvelteKitAuth config ships with debug: true unconditionally, producing verbose auth-flow logging in production.
Several one-line // comments in raid logs page restate the next line of code without adding context.
Five components still use `run` from `svelte/legacy` instead of native `$effect`
Deprecated `$app/stores` still imported in 7 components/routes despite SvelteKit 2 `$app/state` being the new idiom
schema.ts comment claims `unifiedReports` is 'not referenced at runtime' but two FKs still target it
src/lib/utils.ts (file) and src/lib/utils/ (folder) share a name with unrelated contents
Svelte stores split across src/stores.ts and src/lib/stores/ with no rule for which goes where
src/lib/db/ flattens two distinct databases (raid analytics + user/auth) into one 10-file directory
